name: MongoEngine (MongoDB ODM for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); MongoEngine contributors / Harry Marr

auth.notes: MongoDB connection string auth; delegated to pymongo

performance.notes: pymongo backend; ODM layer adds minor overhead vs raw pymongo

agent_readiness.notes: Django-ORM-style ODM for MongoDB. from mongoengine import Document, StringField, IntField, connect. connect('mydb'). class User(Document): name = StringField(required=True); age = IntField(min_value=0); meta = {'collection': 'users'}. user = User(name='Alice', age=30).save(). User.objects(name='Alice').first(). User.objects(age__gte=18). User.objects.filter(name='Alice').order_by('-age'). user.update(set__age=31). User.objects(name='Alice').delete(). EmbeddedDocument: class Address(EmbeddedDocument). ReferenceField for joins. ListField. DictField. Signals. Validation. GridFS.

gotchas: No async support — use Beanie or Motor for FastAPI/asyncio
No type annotations — poor IDE support
Schema migrations not built-in (unlike Django migrations)
Queryset is lazy — iterate or call .first()/.all() to execute
ReferenceField dereferencing can cause N+1 query problems
best_when: Synchronous Flask/Django-style MongoDB apps with schema definition
When Django ORM-style API is familiar and MongoDB is the backend
Document-oriented data with embedded documents and references
Simple CRUD apps with MongoDB and Python
avoid_when: FastAPI or asyncio apps (use Beanie or Motor)
When Pydantic type safety is needed (use Beanie which uses Pydantic)
summary: MongoEngine provides Django-ORM-style ODM for MongoDB. connect('mydb'). class User(Document): name = StringField(). User(name='Alice').save(). User.objects(age__gte=18). filter().order_by(). EmbeddedDocument, ReferenceField, ListField. Signals. Validation. MIT. Best for sync Flask/Django-style MongoDB apps. No async (use Beanie). No type annotations. Schema migrations manual. Lazy querysets. N+1 with ReferenceField.
